item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm guessing that the Foundation cancelled the last test because some higher-up realized the horrific potential for destroying one of the nearby Solar System's celestial bodies instead of the target asteroid by mistake.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't know why but the idea of a Foundation researcher cataloging the contents of a box of complimentary chocolates before dispastionatly throwing them into an incinerator is so charming to me.

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: The image that was used in this article is non-compliant with the SCP wiki's license, as across multiple sources there's no indication this image of a GE Hardiman has been released under Creative Commons. Thus, the image has been removed.

item.title: (Shark) Punching...
item.content:encoded: I think the SPC would be very interested in… borrowing… this particular item :)

item.title: Re: Hmmm.
item.content:encoded:
It successfully crushes SCP-682, but after a few seconds, 682 will begin to slowly "lift" the top bit of 2501 from the wearer's perspective with great strain, and then free themselves. The person wearing SCP-2501 won't be able to close the claw when this is happening.

From an outsiders perspective, SCP-682 looks like they are pushing up nothing and struggling.

item.title: Hmmm.
item.content:encoded:
What happens if we try to use this to squash SCP-682?

(yes, it fails. How does it fail?)

item.title: DAMINALS CRUSH C U P
item.content:encoded: This will make eating these a lot easier

item.title: Re: Provenance
item.content:encoded: I found the image used in an article credited to the museum of science and innovation. I'm not sure if they hold copyright on the image, but if so we might have to ask their permission to use it under their rights and reproductions guidelines.

item.title: Provenance
item.content:encoded:
The image is an engineer testing the gauntlet from General Electric's "Hardiman" exoskeleton prototype. The project was launched in 1965 and canceled in 1971.

I have no idea what the copyright on the image is.

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Timer Expired. Edits made as discussed below. Author may revert at any time.

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team

+ Show Block
- Hide Block

I got a chance to talk to LurkD about some potential crosslinks for this skip. As much fun as it would be to have the claw crush the extra-solar skip of the day, LurkD voted in favor of more world building links, namely a link to the Site-45 Dossier and the MC&D Hub. As such, I am recommending the following edits:

Changing this:

SCP-2501 is to be kept in Storage Vault 3-H at Research Site-45. Only personnel with special exemption keycards will be allowed to access SCP-2501. However, the use and/or misuse of SCP-2501 is still strictly prohibited, punishable up to termination. All previous documentation and research regarding SCP-2501 is to be kept on high security Foundation servers.

To this:

SCP-2501 is to be kept in Storage Vault 3-H at Research Site-45. Only personnel with special exemption keycards will be allowed to access SCP-2501. However, the use and/or misuse of SCP-2501 is still strictly prohibited, punishable up to termination. All previous documentation and research regarding SCP-2501 is to be kept on high security Foundation servers.

Changing this:

SCP-2501 was remitted to a Foundation logistics center in an ornate shipping-case by an anonymous benefactor. Benefactor is confirmed to have close affiliations to Marshall, Carter, and Dark Ltd. as supported by the item description tag attached to SCP-2501 at the time of delivery and a note detailing the context of the parcel.

To this:

SCP-2501 was remitted to a Foundation logistics center in an ornate shipping-case by an anonymous benefactor. Benefactor is confirmed to have close affiliations to Marshall, Carter, and Dark Ltd. as supported by the item description tag attached to SCP-2501 at the time of delivery and a note detailing the context of the parcel.

If anyone has other/better examples that could be used here please leave a comment saying so!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think the gift certificate was more of matter of principal if they did destroy it, but it says "recorded and/or destroyed"- the voucher might have been the "just recorded and stored" part.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
They never said incinerate, they said destroyed, which seems intentionally vague since it could logically include consumption.

"Hey Mitch, whatever happened to those fancy chocolates MC&D sent us with 2501?"
"They were destroyed."
"Destroyed how?"
"They were…pulverized and then dropped into a sack of hydrochloric acid."
"I hate you."

I know most of us hate Lolfoundation, but if these guys didn't eat these chocolates then they're made of stone.

I also question if it was really rational to destroy the gift certificate. How is that anything but an asset?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It was mildly interesting until the note, which made it fantastic. I love things that characterize sinister GoIs as actually reasonable.
The box of chocolates and gift certificate were nice touches.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh I thought the user had to walk up to the object and then crush it directly
I thought 'hey maybe there's a memetic effect that makes the user walk to the object blindly'
Maybe I should have known at the word 'invisible pressure' or the tag 'telekinetic'..

Anyone with the same problem as mine?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Personally, I like the contrast between MC&D sending chocolates with the object, and the Foundation incinerating the chocolates as a matter of course. Both actions are perfectly logical, but show how different MC&D and the Foundation really are.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I laughed freely. This is great. Also, the chocolate is such an MC&D touch that it hurts. Well-played.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oooooh. Kaktus. Can you make me a signature as biyoutiful as that one? Please? Pretty please?

I should NOT have started with this. Take 2.

<sigh> So many excellent SCPs to read. So short a life. <shakes head sadly>

Okay, one nitpick:
I don't give a damn about Buicks and Stuart tanks and weather balloons. Take care of that asteroid RIGHT NOW!!!!!!
Signed: your friendly neighbourhood T-Rex
(Hey Kaktus, can you make me a nice T-Rex signature??????)

+1. May I upvote several times? Please? Just this once?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: i think Perhelion -> Perihelion is right.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If the foundation was more willing to use these SCPs on other stuff….I feel like this might just solve all of their problems tbh?? Like, have you ever imagined using this on the Hateful Star or like…one of those other keter ones that they definitely wanna destroy outright? The only stuff it COULDN'T stop are memetics and such.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: First I was like "Man don't crush a stuart tank those things are awesome" then I was like " WHY THE DIDN'T YOU BLOW UP THE ASTEROID THAT WOULD HAVE BEEN AWESOME". So yeah THE CLAWWWWWWW

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I read it as the lower clearance levels not being told who it was from and that it was just "anonymous"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I feel like I should point out that a signed letter does not equal an anonymous benefactor.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
No images in the forum
thx

I saved this thing to my /b/ folder in 2010.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
*Toy Story alien voice* The claaaaaaaw!

Anyways, good item, good article. My upvote was secured already, and then I saw that remark about the sun and the moon. Talk about fridge horror.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like the idea of MC&D knowing how useful the Foundation is, rather than being completely antagonistic.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
No problem.

Also, since I didn't mention this above, well done. I particularly liked the first half, before the MC&D part, but it's all very good. Easy +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You're absolutley right. I will make that change, thanks.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"SCP-2501 is able to exert an infinite amount of pressure"

That doesn't make much sense. First, don't say infinite amount, that's not a real amount. Second, the resultant objects have varying resultant thicknesses depending on the structural strength of the objects/initial size. This strongly indicates an upper bound on the force/pressure exerted. Something like "No upper limit on the amount of pressure SCP-2501 can exert has yet been observed." would be better.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Perfect example of a simple concept done right. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A simple object, beautifully written. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The MC&D note IS the story. It's not a balls-out beginning-to-end story, but it's a coherent backstory that effectively shows what this does and why it's with the Foundation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I wish you didn't spell out the awful things the gauntlet could do and just left it to fridge logic. Other than that, good job.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The story is that MCD needed to Foundation to take the item off their hands. Their buyers (or potential robbers) had malicious implications to use it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This entire thing is just kind of "meh". It's a thing that does a thing, and the Foundation got it from MCD. So what? Where's the story?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: YES! Another SCP where the Foundation and MC&D treat each other as they ought to; Generally disinterested until it comes to the whole "I kinda like this planet" thing. All my +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I see a lot of new members still writing straight forward anomalous tools/artifacts with little to no catch to them. To those that do so: I urge you to read this. If you want to take a simple concept to the next level without changing things about the object itself, do what this article does. Think of the most interesting ways your concept could be applied to the Foundation universe, and run with it. It'll most likely pay off well.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A Gauntlet of Squishing Your Head. Very series I, but I'm totally up(voting) for it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Couldn't have said it better.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The Headcrusher was exactly what came to mind when I got to the anomalous effects. Despite the way you went out of your way to show how terrifying this item could be, that little voice saying "I'M CRUSHING YOUR HEAD!" makes me almost wish this was a -J.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "Savor those chocolates, they're $70 a box."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I was essentially an upvote the second I saw the image, so long as the article was halfway decent. I was delighted to find that it was equally good.

My headcanon has the chocolates destroyed in the breakroom following thorough examination. X3

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You made a standard, non -J article about the whole "I'm squishing your head" thing? And… it's good? Color me impressed :)

+1

EDIT - That ended up sounding a bit more snarky than I intended too; my bad. Straight up, this is a very well written and interesting article on a core idea that I'd have seen no way not to do a -J on. high five

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Enjoyed seeing the spirit of friendly "let's not end the world" between two groups.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A couple of tone issues, but overall, great job.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Absolutely love this. It's an alright idea executed in a way that doesn't make it boring or a drag to read, and the testing log successfully slowly builds things up in a way that shows the potential of this thing. The MC&D ties make this even better, especially with your interpretation of them and Miss Carter (<3). It feels like a totally natural thing for them to do.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I've got to hand it to you, this had fantastic execution. Way to show how a simple concept can be used to tell a whole story.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's a straightforward item played out interestingly, with a similarly interesting note at the end detailing the interactions between different actors in the SCPverse. Overall a pleasant read.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like the idea that somebody could use it to crush the sun, and MC&D was so afraid of this that they tried to pay off the Foundation to store it.

Other than that, though, there isn't much to it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yes as you so eloquently put, I took a common concept and just ran with it… straight off a cliff.

Feel free to put Headcrusher references here if you feel inclined so we can get them out of the way.

UPDATE: Since djkaktus is a fantastic calligrapher, I've commissioned from him a signature for Lady Carter and included it into the note. Many thanks, kaktus!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Pinchy pinchy
